seo_description: Current time in Auckland, New Zealand — live NZST/NZDT clock, DST schedule, and time differences with Sydney, London, New York, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Auckland right now?
faq.answer: Auckland, New Zealand uses New Zealand Standard Time (NZST, UTC+12) in winter (April–September) and New Zealand Daylight Time (NZDT, UTC+13) during Daylight Saving Time in summer. New Zealand is in the southern hemisphere, so DST runs from the last Sunday in September to the first Sunday in April. The IANA timezone identifier is Pacific/Auckland.

faq.question: What time zone is Auckland in?
faq.answer: Auckland is in the New Zealand Time zone (NZST, UTC+12 in winter; NZDT, UTC+13 in summer). Auckland is one of the world's first major cities to enter each new day, being ahead of most of the world. The Chatham Islands (NZ territory) are even further ahead at UTC+12:45/+13:45. The IANA identifier is Pacific/Auckland.

faq.question: Does Auckland observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. New Zealand observes Daylight Saving Time. Because Auckland is in the southern hemisphere, clocks spring forward on the last Sunday in September (from 2:00 AM to 3:00 AM, UTC+12 to UTC+13) and fall back on the first Sunday in April (from 3:00 AM to 2:00 AM). This is the opposite of Northern Hemisphere DST — Auckland's summer is December–March.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Auckland and Sydney?
faq.answer: Auckland (NZST, UTC+12) is 2 hours ahead of Sydney (AEST, UTC+10) when Sydney is on standard time. During New Zealand's DST (NZDT, UTC+13), Auckland is 3 hours ahead of Sydney on AEST. When both are on DST (Auckland NZDT and Sydney AEDT), the gap reduces to 2 hours. The gap oscillates between 2 and 3 hours depending on DST status.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Auckland and London?
faq.answer: Auckland (NZST, UTC+12) is 12 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in UK winter. During Auckland's DST (NZDT, UTC+13), Auckland is 13 hours ahead of London GMT. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1), the gap is 11–12 hours. The opposing DST seasons create a complex overlap.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Auckland and New York?
faq.answer: Auckland (NZST, UTC+12) is 17 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Auckland is 16 hours ahead. During Auckland's DST (NZDT, UTC+13), the gap increases. Auckland is essentially 'tomorrow' compared to the US East Coast — the same calendar day overlaps only a few hours.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Auckland?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Auckland are 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM NZST/NZDT, Monday through Friday. Auckland is New Zealand's largest city and financial capital, home to the New Zealand Exchange (NZX), which opens at 10:00 AM NZST/NZDT. Auckland's NZDT timezone (UTC+13) makes it one of the first cities globally to open for business each day.
